title: School Admin — Courses & Gradebooks
content:
Overview


The Courses and Gradebooks sections give school admins a bird's-eye view of all academic activity. The All Courses page shows every subject created by teachers. The Enrolments page tracks student enrollment across all subjects. The Gradebook Viewer lets admins inspect any teacher's gradebook for any subject and term. The Assignment Log and Quiz Monitor provide oversight of student work and assessments.



All Courses Page


Displays every subject created by every teacher in the school. Key features:


  • Search: Filter by subject name or teacher name
  • Sort: Click column headers to sort by Subject, Teacher, Student Count, or Creation Date
  • View: See the subject name, assigned teacher, number of enrolled students, and when the course was created
  • Pagination: Navigate through large course lists

This is a read-only view for monitoring purposes. Admins cannot create or edit courses from this page — that is done by teachers in the Teacher Portal. However, admins can see which teachers have created subjects and how many students are enrolled in each.



Enrolments Page


Tracks all student enrolment requests across all subjects. Key features:


  • Term filter: View enrolments by term (Fall, Spring, Winter, Summer, September, January, May)
  • Year selector: Use the plus/minus buttons to change the academic year
  • Status filter: Filter by Pending, Enrolled, or Rejected
  • Search: Find specific students or subjects
  • Sort: Sort by Student, Subject, Teacher, Status, or Date

Each enrolment row shows the student name, subject name, teacher name, current status, and submission date. The status is color-coded: pending (yellow), enrolled (green), rejected (red). Admins can see the full history but approval and rejection are handled by teachers in the Teacher Portal.



Gradebook Viewer


Allows admins to view any teacher's gradebook for any subject and term. This is a read-only inspection tool.


How to Use:


  1. Select a Teacher from the first dropdown — only teachers who have created subjects appear
  2. Select a Subject from the second dropdown — this populates after choosing a teacher and shows only their subjects
  3. Select a Term (Fall Semester, Spring Semester, Winter Semester, September Term, January Term, May Term)
  4. Select the Year using the plus/minus buttons

Once all four selections are made, the gradebook displays a table with:


  • Student names down the left column
  • Grade columns for assessments, midterms, finals, and assignments as entered by the teacher
  • Teacher comments where available — shown inline or via a comment indicator

The gradebook is read-only for admins. To change grades, the teacher must make edits in their own Teacher Portal gradebook.



Assignment Log


Shows all assignments sent by all teachers across the school. Key features:


  • Search: Filter by assignment title, subject, or teacher name
  • Sort: Sort by Title, Subject, Teacher, Student Count, or Date Sent
  • View: See the assignment title, subject, teacher, number of students it was sent to, and when it was sent
  • Pagination: Navigate through large lists

This gives admins visibility into what teachers are assigning and to how many students. It is read-only — assignments are created by teachers in the Teacher Portal.



Quiz Monitor


Tracks self-study quiz attempts by students. Key features:


  • Student selector: Choose a specific student from the dropdown to view their quiz activity
  • Search: Filter the results by quiz name or subject

Once a student is selected, the table shows:


  • Student name
  • Subject the quiz belongs to
  • Quiz name
  • Score achieved
  • Correct count (e.g., 7/10)
  • Date the quiz was taken

This helps admins monitor student engagement with self-study materials and identify students who may need additional support.



Key Talking Points for Agents


  • "The All Courses page is read-only — it shows what teachers have created. You can't add or edit courses from here."
  • "The Gradebook Viewer needs four selections before it loads: teacher, subject, term, and year."
  • "Enrolment approval is done by teachers, not admins. The Enrolments page is for monitoring."
  • "The Quiz Monitor shows self-study quiz attempts — select a student first to see their data."
  • "If a teacher says a grade is wrong, they need to fix it in their own gradebook — admins can view but not edit grades."
